title: Direct injection multipoint nozzle
abstract: An injector including a housing having a fluid channel for fluid and a plurality of injector modules fluidly connected to the fluid channel, where each injector module includes a spray cup having a plurality of radial air passages for directing air radially inwardly into the chamber, and a pressure swirl atomizer having a fluid passage for directing fluid in the axial direction and at least one air passage radially outwardly spaced from the fluid passage for directing air in the axial direction, wherein at least one of the injector modules is axially recessed relative to the other injector modules. By axially recessing at least one of the injector modules, a sheltered burning zone may be provided to enhance flame stability when injecting a mixture of air and fuel from the recessed module.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. An injector including: a housing having a fluid channel for fluid; and a plurality of injector modules fluidly connected to the fluid channel, each injector module including: a spray cup having first and second axial ends, a chamber defined between the ends, and a plurality of radial air passages for directing air radially inwardly into the chamber; and an atomizer including a body having a tip extending into the chamber, a fluid passage extending through the body for directing fluid in the axial direction into the chamber, and at least one air passage radially outwardly spaced from the fluid passage for directing air in the axial direction into the chamber, wherein at least one of the injector modules is axially recessed relative to the other injector modules. 2. The injector according to claim 1 , further including a heatshield assembled to a downstream end of each injector module, the heatshield including a body for protecting the injector modules from combustion heat and a plurality of apertures located so as to allow the fluid from a corresponding injector module to be dispensed from the spray cup, the heatshield and recessed injector module optionally forming a sheltered zone that shelters the fluid from the at least one axially recessed injector module from the other injector modules, the heatshield preferably including a plurality of segments each including a plurality of apertures spaced along a length of the segment in a direction perpendicular to the axial direction, with the segments preferably being oriented in a side-by-side arrangement. 3. The injector according to any preceding claim, wherein the at least one axially recessed injector module includes at least two injector modules axially recessed relative to the other injector modules, the at least two axially recessed injector modules preferably being angled relative to one another, and/or preferably spaced from one another in a direction perpendicular to the axial direction, and/or preferably spaced along a length of the heatshield in a direction perpendicular to the axial direction, and/or preferably spaced from one another in the direction perpendicular to the axial direction along the length of the heatshield such that the injector modules float radially relative to an adjacent one of the plurality of injector modules. 4. The injector according to any preceding claim, wherein each spray cup preferably is monolithic with the atomizer, and includes a plurality of circumferentially spaced radial vanes extending in the axial direction from the first end to the second end, and wherein the plurality of radial air passages are formed between adjacent ones of the plurality of circumferentially spaced radial vanes, and each of the plurality of circumferentially spaced radial vanes preferably has a longitudinal axis and is angled relative to the longitudinal axis, and the spray cup preferably includes an air blast shroud in the chamber that surrounds the at least one air passage and tip of the body. 5. The injector according to any preceding claim, wherein the at least one air passage includes a plurality of circumferentially spaced air passages, and preferably the atomizer includes a plurality of circumferentially spaced axial vanes extending radially outwardly from the body, and wherein the air passages preferably are formed between adjacent ones of the plurality of axial vanes, and preferably each of the plurality of axial vanes has a longitudinal axis and is angled relative to the longitudinal axis to swirl the air in the air passage. 6. The injector according to any preceding claim, wherein body includes an inner portion defining the fluid passage and a plug disposed within the inner portion, wherein one or more swirl passages are defined between the plug and the inner portion to swirl the fluid flowing between the plug and the inner portion, the one or more swirl passages preferably being formed in the inner portion. 7. The injector according to any preceding claim, further including at least one and preferably a plurality of radial struts between adjacent ones of the circumferentially spaced vanes for directing air in the respective radial air passage, wherein the struts preferably are respectively between adjacent ones of the circumferentially spaced vanes, or where the radial strut is a single radial strut extending around the spray cup. 8. An injector module including: a spray cup having first and second axial ends, a chamber defined between the ends, a plurality of circumferentially spaced radial vanes extending in an axial direction from the first end to the second end, and a plurality of circumferentially spaced radial air passages formed between adjacent ones of the circumferentially spaced vanes for directing air radially inwardly into the chamber; and 

 a pressure swirl atomizer including a body having a tip extending into the chamber, a fluid passage extending through the body for directing fluid in the axial direction into the chamber through the tip, and at least one axial air passage radially outwardly spaced from the fluid passage for directing air in the axial direction into the chamber. 9. The injector or injector module according to claim 8, wherein each of the plurality of circumferentially spaced radial vanes has a longitudinal axis and is angled relative to the longitudinal axis, and the spray cup preferably includes an air blast shroud in the chamber that surrounds the at least one air passage and tip of the body, and wherein the spray cup and pressure swirl atomizer preferably are monolithic. 10. The injector or injector module according to any preceding claim, wherein the at least one axial air passage includes a plurality of circumferentially spaced axial air passages, and wherein the pressure swirl atomizer preferably includes a plurality of circumferentially spaced axial vanes extending radially outwardly from the body, and wherein the air passages preferably are formed between adjacent ones of the plurality of axial vanes. 1 1. The injector or injector module according to claim 9 or 10, wherein each of the plurality of axial vanes has a longitudinal axis and is angled relative to the longitudinal axis to swirl the air in the air passage. 12. The injector or injector module according to any preceding claim, wherein the tip of the pressure swirl atomizer is conical, or wherein the spray cup diverges from the first end to the second end. 13. The injector or injector module according to any preceding claim, wherein the air from the radial air passages and the axial air passage combines with the fluid from the fluid passage and is directed out of the spray cup through the second end. 14. The injector or injector module according to any preceding claim, wherein the body includes an inner portion defining the fluid passage, the body preferably including an outer portion surrounding the inner portion, and wherein a heatshield gap preferably is provided between the inner and outer portions that heat shields the fluid in the fluid passage to isolate the fluid from air surrounding the outer portion, and preferably a plug is disposed within the inner portion, and wherein one or more swirl passages preferably are 

 defined between the plug and the inner portion to swirl the fluid flowing between the plug and the inner portion, and the one or more swirl passages preferably is formed in the inner portion. 15. The injector or injector module according to any preceding claim, further including a radial strut between adjacent ones of the circumferentially spaced vanes for directing air in the respective radial air passage, or a plurality of radial struts, wherein the struts are respectively between adjacent ones of the circumferentially spaced vanes, or the radial strut is a single radial strut extending around the spray cup. 16. The injector or injector according to any preceding claim, further including a sheltering shroud extending axially from the second end of the spray cup. 17. A plurality of injector modules according to any of claims 8-16, wherein the plurality of injector modules are spaced from one another in a direction perpendicular to the axial direction such that the injector modules float radially relative to an adjacent one of the plurality of injector modules. 18. A plurality of injector modules configured for fluid connection to a fluid channel, each injector module including: a spray cup having first and second axial ends, a chamber defined between the ends, and a plurality of radial air passages for directing air radially inwardly into the chamber; and a pressure swirl atomizer including a body having a tip extending into the chamber, a fluid passage extending through the body for directing fluid in the axial direction into the chamber, and at least one air passage radially outwardly spaced from the fluid passage for directing air in the axial direction into the chamber, wherein at least one of the injector modules is axially recessed relative to the other injector modules so that fluid from the at least one axially recessed injector module is sheltered from the other injector modules. 19. The injector modules according to claim 18, wherein the at least one axially recessed injector module includes at least two injector modules axially recessed relative to the other injector modules, and the at least two axially recessed injector modules preferably are angled relative to one another, and/or the plurality of injector modules preferably are spaced from one another in a direction perpendicular to the axial direction.
